---
title: "Troubleshooting"
subtitle: "BNR info-hub Technical Manual"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Troubleshooting


## Purpose

This section helps users diagnose and fix common problems in the BNR `info-hub` technical workflow.

It is written for users who are comfortable with Stata, but may be less familiar with Git, Quarto, Typst, PowerShell, and static website publishing.

The normal troubleshooting pathway is:

```text
read the first error -> identify the affected layer -> check paths and files -> fix the smallest problem -> rerun the same command
```

## Start here

| Problem | Use this guide |
|---|---|
| Stata cannot find a file or path | [Fix Stata path errors](stata-path-errors.qmd) |
| A table, figure, metadata file, or build file is missing | [Fix missing output files](missing-output-files.qmd) |
| A download link fails on the website | [Fix broken download links](broken-download-links.qmd) |
| Quarto preview or render fails | [Fix Quarto render errors](quarto-render-errors.qmd) |
| A PDF briefing does not render | [Fix PDF render errors](pdf-render-errors.qmd) |
| Git shows unexpected files | [Clean up Git status](git-status-cleanup.qmd) |

## The three layers to check

```text
Stata compute layer
  scripts/stata/ -> outputs/public/

Publication source layer
  site/ -> site/downloads/files/

Rendered website layer
  site/_site/
```

:::{.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## First rule

Do not start by changing several files at once.

First rerun the exact command that failed. Then read the first error message, not the last one. The first error usually identifies the true problem.
:::

:::{.callout-warning collapse="true"}
## Files that should not be edited manually

Do not manually edit generated outputs unless there is a clear reason and the change is documented.

If a generated file is wrong, update the Stata or Quarto source that created it and regenerate the output.
:::
